title: Serverless Siege — Cloud-Native Red Team
prerequisites: Advanced Offensive Track - Mastery Tier Access
learning_objectives: Cloud-native architecture enumeration and attack surface mapping
Serverless Lambda exploitation: injection, environment variable harvesting, privilege escalation
CI/CD pipeline compromise through GitHub Actions and supply chain attacks
Container environment assessment: ECS task definitions, IAM roles, escape techniques
Cross-service attack chain development for maximum impact
scenario: This Mastery mission requires professional-grade execution in African SaaS company deployed on AWS with serverless architecture. Challenge: Attack cloud-native architecture: Lambda exploitation, CI/CD compromise, cross-service attack chains. Relevance: African cloud adoption accelerating; enterprises lack cloud-native security expertise

scenario_context.setting: African SaaS company deployed on AWS with serverless architecture
scenario_context.challenge: Attack cloud-native architecture: Lambda exploitation, CI/CD compromise, cross-service attack chains
scenario_context.relevance: African cloud adoption accelerating; enterprises lack cloud-native security expertise

subtasks.title: Architecture Enumeration
subtasks.description: Map cloud-native architecture: functions, APIs, containers, CI/CD, IAM.
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Map cloud-native architecture:
        - Functions: What Lambda/Cloud Functions exist?
        - APIs: API Gateway, load balancers, endpoints
        - Containers: ECS/EKS tasks, container registries
        - CI/CD: GitHub Actions, CodePipeline, build systems
        - IAM: Service roles, permissions, trust relationships

        Deliver: Architecture map with attack surface inventory.
subtasks.evidence_required: Architecture map with attack surface inventory

subtasks.title: Serverless Exploitation
subtasks.description: Lambda injection, env harvesting, privilege escalation.
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Exploit Lambda/Functions:
        - Injection: Command injection in Lambda handlers
        - Environment variables: Harvest secrets and credentials
        - Privilege escalation: Exploit service role permissions
        - Lateral movement: Use compromised Lambda to attack other services

        Deliver: Exploitation evidence with IAM escalation path.
subtasks.evidence_required: Serverless exploitation with IAM escalation path

subtasks.title: CI/CD Pipeline Compromise
subtasks.description: GitHub Actions workflow injection or secret extraction.
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Attack build pipeline:
        - GitHub Actions: Workflow injection attacks
        - Secrets extraction: Exfiltrate build secrets
        - Artifact poisoning: Modify build output
        - Supply chain impact: Deliver compromised artifacts

        Deliver: CI/CD compromise with supply chain evidence.
subtasks.evidence_required: CI/CD compromise with supply chain impact

subtasks.title: Container Environment
subtasks.description: ECS task definitions, IAM roles, networking.
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Assess container security:
        - ECS task definitions: Role analysis and secrets
        - Container escape: Break out of container constraints
        - Metadata service: Harvest AWS credentials
        - Networking: Communicate with other containers

        Deliver: Container findings with escape/pivot analysis.

subtasks.title: Cross-Service Attack Chain
subtasks.description: Chain across services for maximum impact.
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Develop multi-service exploitation:
        - Chain across multiple AWS services
        - Maximize impact: Data exfiltration, resource compromise
        - Document complete attack path

        Deliver: Cross-service chain documentation with proof.

subtasks.title: Cloud-Native Assessment
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Create comprehensive 15+ page assessment:
        - Architecture overview and findings
        - Serverless exploitation results
        - CI/CD compromise impact
        - Container and networking findings
        - Cross-service attack chain
        - Architectural recommendations

        Deliver: Cloud-native assessment report (15+ pages).

resources.title: Mission Overview: Serverless Siege — Cloud-Native Red Team

reflection_prompt: How would you prioritize attack surfaces in a serverless architecture deployed across multiple African regions? What supply chain risks exist in CI/CD pipelines serving African SaaS companies?

quiz.question: When enumerating cloud-native architecture for a red team assessment, why is IAM role analysis considered the most critical component?
options: IAM roles are just for human users
Service roles determine what each component can access; misconfigured roles enable lateral movement and privilege escalation across the entire cloud environment
IAM is less important than firewalls
quiz.explanation: IAM roles are the primary security boundary in cloud environments. Service roles define what resources each Lambda, container, or application can access. Misconfigured roles enable operators to pivot across the entire environment, making IAM analysis critical to cloud-native assessment.

quiz.question: In CI/CD pipeline compromise assessments, what is the supply chain impact of successful GitHub Actions workflow injection?
options: Only the immediate build is affected
Successful injection can modify build artifacts, introducing vulnerabilities into production code deployed to millions of users across Africa
CI/CD systems are not real security concerns
Supply chain attacks are not possible in cloud environments
quiz.explanation: CI/CD compromise represents a critical supply chain attack vector. By injecting malicious steps into build workflows, attackers can modify artifacts before deployment. For African SaaS companies, this could affect millions of users across the continent, making CI/CD security assessment essential.

portfolio_artifacts: Evidence of technical excellence and strategic thinking
mentor_notes:
This Mastery mission requires senior mentor review. Candidates must demonstrate:
- Professional-grade execution and documentation
- Deep understanding of African organizational and threat contexts
- Strategic thinking beyond tactical execution
- Readiness for commercial red team operations
- Leadership potential within OCH and broader community

Successful completion positions candidate for Mastery portfolio and professional practice.
